compiled_state.title: Ticket search: stale local tickets branch caused false-negative epic lookup
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
#### 1. Technical Environment
* **Context Scope:** /home/user/digital-service-orchestra, branch claude/fix-jira-status-sync-1qMqU
* **Active Configs:** tickets branch mounted at .tickets-tracker/ as git worktree

#### 2. Incident Overview
* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior
* **Expected Behavior:** Searching for an epic referencing "worktree" should have found dso-to7x ("Allow coding sub-agents to use worktree isolation")
* **Actual Behavior:** Three separate search approaches all returned no results, and the agent confidently reported no such epic existed. The user corrected this.

#### 3. Action History & Workarounds
* **Steps Taken:**
  * Action 1: .claude/scripts/dso ticket list 2>&1 | grep -i worktree → no output
  * Action 2: .claude/scripts/dso ticket list 2>&1 | python3 (search title, description, comments for "worktree") → no output
  * Action 3: ls -d */ in .tickets-tracker/ → only 10 directories (newly created tickets)
  * Action 4: grep -rl "worktree" and Grep tool on .tickets-tracker/ → no matches
* **Raw Results:** All four commands returned empty results. The ticket list only contained 10 tickets created in the current session. The full list (with 30+ tickets including dso-to7x) only appeared after git pull origin tickets --rebase was run later to push ticket changes.
* **Known Workarounds:** Running git pull origin tickets --rebase before searching would have synced the local tickets branch with remote and surfaced all tickets.

#### 4. Chronological Rationalization
* Step 1: Ran ticket list piped through grep — got no results, assumed tickets were fully loaded
* Step 2: Ran broader python search across all ticket fields — still nothing, confidence increased that no such epic existed
* Step 3: Manually listed .tickets-tracker/ directories and searched files — only saw 10 dirs, reinforcing the false conclusion
* Step 4: Reported to user that no worktree epic existed
* Rule Check: The agent did not verify that the local tickets branch was in sync with remote before searching. The tickets branch had a stuck rebase that was aborted earlier in the session, leaving it behind the remote and missing most ticket data. The agent treated the incomplete local state as authoritative.

#### 5. Corrective Actions Needed
* Agent documentation should instruct the agent to verify the tickets branch is up-to-date with remote (git fetch + check) before performing ticket searches, especially after rebase operations on the tickets branch.
* The ticket CLI (or a wrapper) could warn when the local tickets branch is behind the remote.
* Consider adding a staleness check to the ticket list command itself.

comments.body: Investigation confirmed. Fix applied in this session.
comments.author: Test
